% ======================================================================
% Table 3: Individual Rule Performance
% ======================================================================
% Key messages:
%   (1) No universal rule: best shifts from Standby-Lazy (S/M/XL) to Charge-High (L)
%   (2) Large inter-rule gap: 2.0x--2.6x between best and worst per scale
%   (3) Equivalent rules: †STTF=EDD=HPF, ‡Charge-High=Charge-Opp, §Standby-LC=Standby-HM
%   (4) Low-cost rules reject heavily: Rej>=10 in red; RL-APC Rej in bold (near-zero)
%   (5) RL-APC beats all rules only in S: bold cost only for S-scale
\begin{table*}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Individual rule performance across four problem scales (30 test instances each).
For each rule--scale combination, we report average total cost and number of rejected tasks (Rej).
Rules are grouped by category; identical superscripts denote equivalent rules
($^\dagger$\,STTF\,=\,EDD\,=\,HPF;
 $^\ddagger$\,Charge-High\,=\,Charge-Opp;
 $^\S$\,Standby-LC\,=\,Standby-HM),
reducing 15 rules to 11 effectively independent strategies.
\underline{Underlined}: best single-rule cost per scale.
\textbf{Bold} RL-APC cost: lower than all single rules.
Rej in \textcolor{red}{red}: $\geq 10$ rejected tasks.
The best rule shifts from Standby-Lazy (S, M, XL) to Charge-High (L),
and the cost ratio between worst and best single rule ranges from
$2.0\times$ (XL) to $2.6\times$ (S).}
\label{tab:rules}
\small
\begin{tabular}{ll rr rr rr rr}
\toprule
 & & \multicolumn{2}{c}{S} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{M} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{L} & \multicolumn{2}{c}{XL} \\
\cmidrule(lr){3-4}\cmidrule(lr){5-6}\cmidrule(lr){7-8}\cmidrule(lr){9-10}
Rule & Category & Cost & Rej & Cost & Rej & Cost & Rej & Cost & Rej \\
\midrule
% --- Dispatch rules ---
STTF$^\dagger$ & Dispatch & 110,551 & 7.9 & 294,978 & \textcolor{red}{26.5} & 491,192 & \textcolor{red}{45.8} & 764,969 & \textcolor{red}{74.0} \\
EDD$^\dagger$ & Dispatch & 110,551 & 7.9 & 294,978 & \textcolor{red}{26.5} & 491,192 & \textcolor{red}{45.8} & 764,969 & \textcolor{red}{74.0} \\
MST & Dispatch & 108,707 & 7.5 & 294,407 & \textcolor{red}{26.2} & 490,298 & \textcolor{red}{45.8} & 768,743 & \textcolor{red}{74.3} \\
HPF$^\dagger$ & Dispatch & 110,551 & 7.9 & 294,978 & \textcolor{red}{26.5} & 491,192 & \textcolor{red}{45.8} & 764,969 & \textcolor{red}{74.0} \\
Insert-MC & Dispatch & 103,048 & 2.7 & 351,911 & \textcolor{red}{16.0} & 447,292 & \textcolor{red}{36.4} & 747,761 & \textcolor{red}{46.1} \\
\addlinespace
% --- Charge rules ---
Charge-Urgent & Charge & 118,337 & 8.2 & 301,809 & \textcolor{red}{26.2} & 498,087 & \textcolor{red}{45.5} & 841,829 & \textcolor{red}{66.8} \\
Charge-Low & Charge & 118,135 & 8.2 & 335,128 & \textcolor{red}{21.7} & 737,186 & \textcolor{red}{14.7} & 1,141,406 & \textcolor{red}{34.0} \\
Charge-Med & Charge & 112,644 & 7.5 & 299,669 & \textcolor{red}{25.9} & 490,829 & \textcolor{red}{43.1} & 915,014 & \textcolor{red}{57.3} \\
Charge-High$^\ddagger$ & Charge & 83,962 & 1.4 & 266,218 & \textcolor{red}{20.8} & \underline{336,386} & \textcolor{red}{16.8} & 790,373 & \textcolor{red}{55.0} \\
Charge-Opp$^\ddagger$ & Charge & 83,962 & 1.4 & 266,218 & \textcolor{red}{20.8} & 336,386 & \textcolor{red}{16.8} & 790,373 & \textcolor{red}{55.0} \\
\addlinespace
% --- Standby rules ---
Standby-LC$^\S$ & Standby & 126,546 & 9.2 & 301,201 & \textcolor{red}{26.3} & 497,103 & \textcolor{red}{45.3} & 762,554 & \textcolor{red}{74.3} \\
Standby-Lazy & Standby & \underline{60,731} & 3.8 & \underline{224,316} & \textcolor{red}{19.2} & 418,299 & \textcolor{red}{37.9} & \underline{696,148} & \textcolor{red}{64.6} \\
Standby-HM$^\S$ & Standby & 126,546 & 9.2 & 301,201 & \textcolor{red}{26.3} & 497,103 & \textcolor{red}{45.3} & 762,554 & \textcolor{red}{74.3} \\

Accept-Val & Accept & 157,360 & 0.0 & 490,642 & 0.0 & 831,429 & 0.0 & 1,360,537 & 0.0 \\
\midrule
\textbf{RL-APC} & \textbf{Adaptive} & \textbf{39,846} & \textbf{0.0} & 286,665 & \textbf{0.0} & 662,267 & \textbf{0.0} & 814,344 & \textbf{1.5} \\

% ======================================================================
% Table 4: RL-APC vs Best Fixed Rule (multi-dimensional)
% ======================================================================
\begin{table*}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Multidimensional comparison of RL-APC against the best-performing fixed rule (by cost) for each scale. Wilcoxon signed-rank tests on 30 paired instances. Cost/task = average cost per completed task. While the best fixed rule achieves lower total cost in M/L/XL, it does so by rejecting a large fraction of incoming tasks (see Rej column).}
\label{tab:rl_vs_best}
\small
\begin{tabular}{llrrrrrrrc}
\toprule
 & & \multicolumn{3}{c}{Best Fixed Rule} & \multicolumn{3}{c}{RL-APC} & & \\
\cmidrule(lr){3-5}\cmidrule(lr){6-8}
Scale & Best Rule & Cost & Compl & Rej & Cost & Compl & Rej & $\Delta_\text{cost}$ (\%) & $p$ \\
\midrule
S & Standby-Lazy & 60,731 & 12.4 & 3.8 & \textbf{39,846} & 14.5 & \textbf{0.0} & $-34.4$** & $5.38e-03$ \\
M & Standby-Lazy & \textbf{224,316} & 13.8 & \textcolor{red}{19.2} & 286,665 & 18.1 & \textbf{0.0} & $+27.8$** & $2.77e-03$ \\
L & Charge-High & \textbf{336,386} & 30.4 & \textcolor{red}{16.8} & 662,267 & 17.5 & \textbf{0.0} & $+96.9$*** & $9.31e-09$ \\
XL & Standby-Lazy & \textbf{696,148} & 18.8 & \textcolor{red}{64.6} & 814,344 & 22.2 & \textbf{1.5} & $+17.0$ & $0.058$ \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table*}

% Table 7: Online vs Offline — Gap Analysis

\begin{table}[htbp]

M & 286,665 & 281,349 & 53,600 & $+434.8$ & $+424.9$ \\
L & 662,267 & 336,386 & — & — & — \\

% ======================================================================
% Table 8: Cost Decomposition — Weighted Cost Contributions
% ======================================================================
\begin{table*}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Cost decomposition into weighted components (averages over 30 instances). Travel = $C_\text{tr} \times d + C_\text{time} \times t$; Charging = $C_\text{ch} \times t_\text{ch}$; Tardiness = $C_\text{delay} \times \tau$; Idle = $C_\text{wait} \times t_w + C_\text{cf} \times (t_w + t_\text{cf}) + C_\text{sb} \times t_\text{sb}$; Rejection = $10{,}000 \times n_\text{rej}$; Other = terminal penalties and reward shaping. Rejection cost (in \textcolor{red}{red} when $\geq 30\%$ of total) dominates baselines; RL-APC shifts cost to idle (conservative waiting).}
\label{tab:cost_decomp}
\small
\begin{tabular}{llrrrrrrrr}
\toprule
Scale & Method & Travel & Charg. & Tard. & Idle & Reject. & Other & Total & \% Rej. \\
\midrule
S & RL-APC & 1,487 & 78 & 273 & 3,323 & 0 & 34,684 & 39,846 & 0\% \\
 & Greedy-FR & 8,729 & 1,110 & 34,274 & 4,279 & 15,333 & 31,047 & 94,772 & 16\% \\
 & Standby-Lazy & 1,053 & 9 & 0 & 2,982 & \textcolor{red}{38,000} & 18,688 & 60,731 & 63\% \\
\addlinespace
M & RL-APC & 3,123 & 199 & 44 & 3,629 & 0 & 279,671 & 286,665 & 0\% \\
 & Greedy-FR & 4,087 & 336 & 1,210 & 6,182 & \textcolor{red}{218,000} & 51,535 & 281,349 & 77\% \\
 & Standby-Lazy & 1,686 & 6 & 0 & 4,440 & \textcolor{red}{192,333} & 25,851 & 224,316 & 86\% \\
\addlinespace
L & RL-APC & 4,598 & 276 & 11,487 & 7,322 & 0 & 638,584 & 662,267 & 0\% \\
